rationale_top3:
Families come back to BIG4 Gold Coast year after year. Kids refuse to leave, parents actually relax, and the heated waterpark rivals anything on the Gold Coast. A resort pool, jumping pillow, flying fox, daily activity program and the Nibbles Café with poolside ordering round out the on-site experience. Movie World is a 3-minute drive from the front gate.

One thing to know: request sites toward the riverside or interior of the park to avoid highway noise on the M1 side.
rationale_honourable: Well-landscaped grounds balancing resort amenities with green spaces. Close to all major Gold Coast theme parks while maintaining a peaceful retreat atmosphere. Comprehensive dog facilities including daycare, wash stations, and off-leash areas.
water_fun: Massive heated waterpark with multiple slides, resort pool, spa
kids_play: Jumping pillow, playground, flying fox, daily activities, games room
pet_detail: Dog-friendly with on-site doggy daycare (Goobers), large off-leash dog park, dog wash facilities, and dogs welcome at Nibbles Cafe outdoor area.
best_for: Active families with children who want a full-featured resort experience with world-class waterpark facilities, premium amenities, and direct theme park access without sacrificing the camping/caravan park atmosphere.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Gold Coast Holiday Park
SCORE: 92/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 424

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Reviews consistently describe the heated waterpark as "next level," "unreal," and "absolutely amazing" with multiple slides, lifeguards on every level, and splash zones keeping kids entertained for hours. Daily activities include cupcake decorating, train rides, build-a-bear, movie nights, circus activities, and hair braiding. The jumping pillow, flying fox, games room, and organized kids' entertainment create what families call "a true family resort" where "kids would rather stay at the park instead of going to Movie World."

Nature (13/20) — The park features lovely gardens described as "lush" and "beautifully maintained" with tropical landscaping throughout. A creek runs along one section with a riverside walk where guests spot koalas in trees near the playground. However, the park is primarily a manicured resort setting rather than natural bushland, located in suburban Helensvale adjacent to the M1 motorway, with limited natural environment beyond the maintained grounds.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Amenities are repeatedly described as "spotless," "immaculate," and "the best we have ever seen" with unique tropical fish tank features, music in bathrooms, large shower caddies, and full-length mirrors. Multiple reviews note amenities are "cleaned spotlessly numerous times a day" and everything is "beautifully maintained." A small number of accommodation maintenance issues were reported (flickering lights, mould in one cabin, housekeeping inconsistencies) preventing a perfect score.

Value (14/15) — Families consistently report "great value for money" and that "the water park and pool alone are worth the site fees." The on-site cafe offers "well priced" food with happy hour cocktails from $8-12, and theme park ticket discounts are available. Some guests noted prices are "a bit pricey" particularly during peak season, and cafe prices received occasional comments, but overwhelming sentiment is that the comprehensive facilities justify the cost.

Site Size (9/10) — Powered sites are repeatedly described as "huge," "spacious," "generously sized," and "super generous" with families reporting ample room for awnings, play areas, and dining spaces. Premium Evergreen synthetic turf sites are particularly praised for their size and flatness. A few reviews mentioned sites feeling "a little close together" and unpowered sites being "miles away from facilities," preventing a perfect score.

Sentiment (10/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with exceptional repeat visitation rates—numerous families reporting 4th, 5th, 6th, and 7th visits. Reviews consistently include phrases like "will definitely be back," "can't wait to return," "already booked again," and "this is our go-to." Guests describe feeling "sad to leave" and children asking "when can we come back." The park clearly exceeds expectations with families stating it has "set the bar ridiculously high" for future holidays.

Location (10/10) — Location is universally praised as "perfect" and "unbeatable"—literally across from Movie World with 2-5 minute drive to Wet'n'Wild and Dreamworld. Walking distance to theme parks (15-20 minutes), bus stop directly outside for theme park access, close to Westfield shopping, fast food options, and indoor go-karting next door. Easy M1 motorway access makes it "close to everything" while still feeling like a holiday destination.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Highway noise from adjacent M1 motorway is "next level horrendous" with trucks and traffic audible from 4am, particularly affecting sites near the front of the park, though accommodation further back is well-insulated.
2. Limited natural environment—while gardens are lovely, the park lacks bushland, beach access, or significant natural features beyond a small creek area, functioning more as a resort than nature-based camping experience.
3. Minor accommodation maintenance issues reported including mould in bathroom window seals, flickering lights, inconsistent housekeeping service, broken beds in one cabin, and some bungalows needing paint and maintenance updates.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids would rather stay at the park instead of going to Movie world," "water park is next level," "amenities are spotless," "staff are absolute legends," "best caravan park we have stayed," "don't need to leave the park," "magic in every stay," "kids having the time of their lives"

rationale_top3:
Broadwater sits right on the water with waterfront sites, direct beach access and a genuine community feel. Free pancake breakfasts and daily swans keep families coming back for decades. Two heated pools, a jumping pillow, playground and a packed activity program including outdoor movies and kids disco nights keep everyone busy. The light rail connects you to the rest of the coast.

One thing to know: ask for waterfront or park-facing sites. No WiFi on site.
rationale_honourable: Direct Broadwater beach access and waterfront positioning. Good value considering the extensive facilities and entertainment program included.
water_fun: Two pools (one heated), direct Broadwater access, boat ramp
kids_play: Large jumping pillow, playground, games room, holiday activities
best_for: Best for families with young children who want a premium waterfront experience with extensive kids' facilities, clean amenities, and proximity to Gold Coast theme parks, particularly those who value organized activities and a strong sense of community
executive_summary:
PARK: Broadwater Tourist Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 205

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Exceptional facilities with two swimming pools (heated in winter), large covered jumping pillow, playground, games room, and organized holiday activities including free pancake breakfasts, kids disco nights, outdoor movie screenings, sausage sizzles, and regular food trucks (pizza and coffee vans). Multiple families praised the "heaps to do" for kids and the school holiday programs.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding waterfront location directly on the Broadwater with private beach access, boat ramp, and fish-cleaning station. Guests consistently praised the water views, maritime activity, wildlife including visiting swans and ducks, and adjacent public parklands with walking tracks. The park offers easy beach walks, swimming, and fishing right from the shore.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly praised as "spotlessly clean" with amenities "cleaned at least 3 times a day" and described as "immaculate." Multiple reviews specifically mentioned maintenance staff visiting amenities at 5am to restock supplies, beautifully manicured lawns maintained daily, and facilities being the cleanest families had encountered across multiple park stays.

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback with many praising "great value" and reasonable pricing for the location and facilities, but several reviews mentioned prices "starting to get a bit high" and being "a little bit expensive." Most agreed the quality and location justified the cost, though some self-contained travellers questioned value when not using amenities.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive with "reasonably good sized sites," "good sized sites," and "spacious" mentioned frequently. Some challenges noted for larger rigs (26ft+ vans) in certain areas, with a few reviews mentioning sites being "tight" for 9m+ setups with bike racks. Waterfront and ensuite sites received particular praise for size and quality.

Sentiment (9/10) — Exceptional repeat visitation with families reporting 8-22 years of annual returns, multi-generational bookings, and overwhelming recommendations. Phrases like "our favourite," "can't wait to come back," "magical place," and "THE BEST" appear throughout. Staff (especially managers Brooke and Matt) receive consistent praise for creating a welcoming "home" atmosphere.

Location (5/10) — Excellent proximity to Gold Coast attractions (SeaWorld, theme parks 10-15 minutes away), Australia Fair shopping centre (10-minute walk), light rail at the gate, restaurants, and Surfers Paradise (15 minutes). However, close proximity to Southport results in frequent emergency sirens throughout the day. Some road noise from Marine Parade affects sites near the promenade. Being slightly removed from main Surfers Paradise beach means it's quieter but requires transport for main beach access.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Location impacts from being in Southport include constant emergency sirens "several times a day" and significant road noise from Marine Parade affecting sites closest to the promenade (6am-8pm), as noted in multiple reviews.
2. WiFi is non-functional and has been broken for 2+ years across Gold Coast Tourist Parks properties according to multiple reviews, with guests describing this as "fake advertising" and requesting it become "a priority to get working."
3. Site size challenges exist for larger rigs (26ft+), with some families reporting difficulty maneuvering into allocated spaces and needing to relocate or remove bike racks, indicating layout constraints for modern large caravans.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean, cleaned at least 3 times a day," "been coming here for 22 years," "staff go above and beyond," "kids love it so much for them to do," "right on the Broadwater with perfect views," "beautifully manicured grounds," "free pancake breakfast was a special touch," "can't wait to come back," "our favourite Gold Coast park," "magical place," "close to everything yet far enough from hustle and bustle," "THE BEST"

rationale_top3: Exceptional waterfront location directly on Tallebudgera Creek with safe swimming areas and short walk to beach access. Outstanding family entertainment facilities including heated pool with fun slides, adults-only pool, and diverse water activity options. High cleanliness standards and positive guest sentiment with friendly staff and well-maintained amenities.
rationale_honourable: Convenient proximity to Burleigh Heads and Gold Coast attractions. Good site sizing for family camping needs. Reasonable value proposition with on-site food truck and holiday activities.
water_fun: Heated pools with waterslides plus creek swimming
kids_play: Pool slides, playground, rental trikes, holiday activities
pet_detail: NO DOGS - explicitly stated as not pet-friendly by reviewers
best_for: Families seeking a feature-rich waterfront park with excellent swimming options (pool and creek), modern amenities, and prime Gold Coast location, who don't mind paying premium prices for a top-tier experience
executive_summary:
PARK: Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-18
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 355

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Consistently praised for heated pools with waterslides that keep kids entertained for hours, rental trikes/bikes, holiday activities during peak periods, and the natural entertainment of creek access for paddle boarding, fishing, and swimming. Food trucks and on-site kiosk add convenience. Some mention playground needs updating.

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural setting directly on Tallebudgera Creek with safe enclosed swimming areas, walking distance to surf beaches, coastal walks to Burleigh Heads National Park, and beautiful sunrise/sunset views. Wildlife sightings include rainbow lorikeets, iguanas, bush turkeys. Creek described as clear and beautiful with sandy banks.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Amenities frequently described as "spotless," "always clean," and "very well maintained" with staff cleaning regularly throughout the day. However, some negative reviews cite issues with corroded shower heads, broken toilet seats not fixed promptly, mould in cabin bathrooms, and occasional cleanliness lapses during peak periods.

Value (11/15) — Pricing described as "expensive," "overpriced," and premium with unpowered sites $70-100/night and cabins $400+/night. Many feel the location justifies the cost, but some express disappointment at value given aging cabin conditions and maintenance issues. Described as "paying for the location."

Site Size (9/10) — Powered sites consistently praised as "generous," "really nice sized," "extremely large," and "good sized sites for larger caravans" with easy drive-through access. Some complaints about unpowered tent sites being "tiny" and cramped near highway.

Sentiment (9/10) — Extremely high repeat visitation with families returning for 7-55 years annually. Described as "home away from home," "second home," "favourite place." However, several recent negative reviews cite rude management (specifically "Kate"), poor customer service recovery, and feeling unwelcome, preventing a perfect score.

Location (10/10) — Perfect marks for location directly on creek with underpass to Palm Beach, 5-10 minute walk to surf beach and Burleigh Heads, walking distance to Burleigh village cafes/restaurants, and easy access to Gold Coast attractions. Public transport at park entrance. Central Gold Coast position.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Cabins showing significant age with maintenance issues including mould in bathrooms, broken fixtures, dated interiors, and inconsistent cleaning standards despite premium pricing ($400+/night).
2. Customer service concerns with multiple reviews citing rude management (particularly "Kate"), inflexible policies, poor complaint resolution, and guests feeling unwelcome or dismissed when raising legitimate issues.
3. Cleanliness lapses during peak periods, maintenance items not addressed promptly (broken toilets/showers for weeks), and amenities needing renovation despite generally good upkeep and frequent cleaning efforts.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"perfect for families," "amazing staff, great amenities, cleanliness," "best location right on the creek," "heated pool with fun slides," "spotless and very well maintained," "home away from home," "unbeatable location," "safe swimming areas," "staff are rude and unprofessional," "cabins are outdated, dirty, bathrooms full of mould," "overpriced," "worst customer service," "Kate was dismissive and rude," "everything is right at your doorstep"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards and immaculate presentation throughout the park Outstanding beach location with patrolled beach access just steps away from sites Hosts Bec and Pete provide exceptional service and regularly organised activities including pancake breakfasts, movie nights, and arts and crafts
rationale_honourable: Safe, family-friendly environment where children can ride scooters freely around the park Charming boutique park atmosphere with fairy garden and kids corner features Good old fashioned camping experience with strong community feel
water_fun: No pool; beach across road main attraction
kids_play: Fairy garden, dinosaur area, organised daily activities
pet_detail: No pets allowed at this park
best_for: Families with children under 10 who prioritize beach access, exceptional cleanliness, and community atmosphere over resort-style facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Ocean Beach Tourist Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-16
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 110

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (15/20) — Reviews consistently praise management-organized activities including pancake breakfasts, movie nights with popcorn and marshmallows, arts and crafts, sausage sizzles, rock painting, and koala chocolate hunts. Families highlight the "fairy garden and little Jurassic Park dinosaur area" where 3-year-olds "spent hours playing every day." Multiple reviews note managers "lend scooters/bikes" and create daily activities during school holidays that compensate for no pool or games room.

Nature (19/20) — Exceptional beachfront location with "direct access to supervised Miami South Beach" literally across the road through a back gate. Families describe patrolled swimming areas, shallow water perfect for kids, abundant shells, whale watching opportunities, and "the sound of waves crashing" at night. The park is "surrounded by lush bushland and sheltered by the headland" with "amazing beachfront walks in some of the best beachfront parklands in Australia."

Cleanliness (15/15) — Overwhelming consensus on exceptional cleanliness. Multiple families who have "travelled all around Australia" call this "the cleanest bins and laundry facilities we have seen anywhere in the country." Reviews repeatedly use "sparkling clean," "immaculately cleaned every day," "spotless amenities," and "honestly sparkles like it's brand new" to describe bathrooms, camp kitchen, and laundry facilities.

Value (12/15) — Pricing at $62-69 per night receives mixed feedback. Some families consider it "extremely affordable" and "worth the money," especially given the beachfront location. However, multiple reviews note it's "pricey" for the Gold Coast, with one family paying $120/night feeling "not satisfied with value" after experiencing a power cut and WiFi issues. Most agree location justifies cost but some "would not stay again" based on price.

Site Size (8/10) — Sites described as "quite spacious," "very generous," and "nice and big" with many featuring artificial turf and concrete pads. However, the small 62-site park has "narrow roadways" and is "a little tight in places" making it "tight to get into the site or out" with larger rigs. One critical review called it "a glorified carpark" though most families found site sizes adequate for their needs.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional repeat visitation with families returning for 4-6+ years consecutively. Reviews use phrases like "our favourite park," "home away from home," "kids begging to come back," and "we will keep coming back." The park "totally deserved winning the 2025 ReviewPro People's Choice Caravan Park of the Year Award and officially being named the #1 caravan park in the country." Very high recommendation rate among family reviewers.

Location (10/10) — Perfect Gold Coast location directly on Miami Beach with patrolled surf club and cafe literally at the gate. "Walking distance to Aldi, Coles, Dan Murphy's, restaurants, and 6 playgrounds" while remaining "tucked away and peaceful." Easy bike/Lime scooter access to Burleigh Heads and Surfers Paradise (15-30 minutes). Public transport connections directly in front. "Park your car once and literally never move it again."

WHY NOT 100:
1. No WiFi or reliable mobile service creates significant frustration for modern families, with multiple reviews calling it a "Telstra black hole" and noting inability to contact reception during emergencies.
2. No pool or traditional playground facilities means the park entirely depends on beach access for entertainment, limiting appeal during poor weather or for families wanting diverse activity options.
3. Value concerns with pricing at $68-120/night considered high by multiple reviewers, particularly when experiencing issues like lukewarm showers, power cuts without explanation, or bikes riding through campsites.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"cleanest bins and laundry facilities anywhere in the country," "fairy garden and Jurassic Park dinosaur area," "pancake breakfasts and movie nights," "park your car once and never move it," "won #1 caravan park in the country," "truly feels like a family community," "directly across from beautiful Miami Beach," "managers go way above and beyond"

park_name: NRMA Treasure Island Holiday Resort, Gold Coast
rationale_top3:
Treasure Island packs in multiple pools, water slides, a splash pad, jumping pillow and mini golf, with Dreamworld next door and Movie World minutes away. Facilities are well-maintained and consistently reliable.

One thing to know: sites run smaller than BIG4 and the park gets busy in school holidays. Book early for peak periods.
rationale_honourable: Good value given the extensive onsite facilities, quality bistro dining, and walking distance to major shopping and attractions despite compact site sizing.
water_fun: Multiple pools, waterslides, splash pad, heated spas
kids_play: Jumping pillow, mini golf, daily activities, wreck room
pet_detail: Pet friendly with designated off-leash area, restrictions apply to communal areas and during peak season
best_for: This park is perfect for families with children under 12 who want entertainment at their doorstep and easy access to Gold Coast theme parks without needing to venture far from base.
executive_summary:
PARK: NRMA Treasure Island Holiday Resort, Gold Coast
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 360

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Exceptional on-site entertainment with multiple pools including waterslides, pirate-themed splash park, jumping pillow, mini golf, tennis/basketball courts, go-karts for hire, and daily kids activities run by enthusiastic staff. Reviews consistently praise the variety of activities: "so much to do for the kids", "kids loved the waterslide and jumping pillow", "didn't have to leave the park". Weekend activities include movie nights on big screen, trivia, bingo, and kids club with crafts. Only minor deductions for some activities not running during non-peak periods.

Nature (11/20) — The park is surrounded by retail outlets in an industrial estate setting with limited natural environment. Reviews note it's "surrounded by large retail outlets", "in the middle of an industrial retail estate", and "far from the beach" requiring a 5-minute drive or 30-minute bus/tram ride to reach the coast. While the park has lovely gardens and tropical plantings with bush turkeys and possums visible, there's no direct beach access or significant bushland. The nearby Biggera Creek provides some natural setting for ensuite sites at the rear.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Mixed feedback on cleanliness with many praising "great clean facilities", "spotless rooms", "amenities were clean", and "cleanest facilities of any holiday park". However, numerous recent reviews note declining standards: "toilets and showers were constantly dirty during our 3 night stay", "rubbish through the splash pad", "undies in the spas", "German cockroaches", "thick dust on ceiling fans", "black mould in air conditioning units", and "filthy floors in safari tent". Maintenance issues mentioned include broken locks, dated facilities, and outdoor furniture in poor condition.

Value (11/15) — Reviews show mixed value perception. Many find it reasonable: "great value", "$68 a night for powered site", "affordable meals", "reasonably priced". However, numerous complaints about pricing: "$100 per night... absolutely ridiculous", "nearly $400 a night I expected more", "ridiculously overpriced" food ($6 ice cream, $5 soft drink with 15% surcharge, $24 cocktails, $17 for 2 coffees). Several note the park is "expensive for what was offered" and facilities need upgrading for the prices charged. Peak season pricing particularly criticized.

Site Size (6/10) — Consistent complaints about tight caravan sites: "sites are tight", "a little tight", "very tight to back into", "postage stamp size caravan site", "site was too small for our van", "small site with each site crammed in", "couldn't put the van annex out", "difficult to reverse van in". Powered sites noted as barely big enough for tent and car. Premium sites described as adequate but ensuite sites vary significantly in size. Cabins and villas received better space reviews as "spacious" and "roomy".

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive family sentiment with high repeat visitation: "will definitely be back", "stayed multiple times", "our families favourite place in the world", "best caravan park we have been in", "kids didn't want to leave", "3rd Halloween in a row", "coming here for YEARS". Strong recommendation rate evident. However, some families stating "this will be our last" due to declining standards, and a few very negative experiences with customer service and cleanliness issues prevent a perfect score.

Location (10/10) — Excellent location scores with reviews praising proximity to everything: "walking distance to Harbour Town", "Coles just over the road", "close to all theme parks" (15-20 minutes to SeaWorld, Movie World, Wet'n'Wild), "central to everything", "short drive to Surfers Paradise" (10-20 minutes). Bus stop at front gate, easy freeway access. Only negative is distance from beach, but convenience to shopping and attractions compensates significantly.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site sizes are consistently too small for larger caravans with multiple reviews describing difficult reversing, inability to fit awnings, and cramped conditions that detract significantly from the camping experience.
2. Cleanliness and maintenance standards have declined with recent reviews reporting dirty amenities, pest issues (cockroaches), mould in air conditioning, worn furniture, and general dated facilities that need urgent attention and investment.
3. Value concerns are significant with overpriced food/beverages on-site, high accommodation costs for dated facilities, and families feeling the park doesn't justify premium pricing without facility upgrades, particularly during peak seasons.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best caravan park in Australia for families", "so much to do for the kids", "staff were absolutely fantastic", "sites were a little tight", "facilities looking tired and in need of update", "walking distance to Harbour Town", "kids loved the waterslide and jumping pillow", "didn't have to leave the park", "ridiculously overpriced", "close to all theme parks", "rooms could do with an update", "cleanest facilities of any holiday park"

rationale_top3: Entertainment facilities including pool with shallow area and well-equipped games room score 15.0, providing excellent family-focused amenities Spotless cleanliness standards score 14.0, with consistently praised amenities and presentation creating a safe, tidy environment Strong nature accessibility score 13.0, with beach directly across the road offering coastal experiences without leaving the urban convenience
rationale_honourable: Positive sentiment score of 10.0 driven by friendly, helpful staff and well-maintained smoke-free environment that families appreciate for safety and comfort
water_fun: Nice pool with shallow step area for littlies
kids_play: Games room, Easter activities, playground removed recently
pet_detail: No dogs allowed (smoke-free and pet-free park)
best_for: Families with young children seeking a clean, safe, beachfront park with excellent access to cafes, surf club, and Gold Coast attractions via walking or tram.
executive_summary:
PARK: Main Beach Tourist Park
SCORE: 76/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 103

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (13/20) — Reviews highlight a well-equipped games room, pool with shallow areas for young children, and special events like Easter activities, food trucks, coffee vans, and live music organized by management. One recent review noted disappointment that the small playground was removed after closure, leaving only the pool for on-site kids entertainment beyond the games room.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional beach access directly across the road from Main Beach with patrolled swimming and surf club access. Multiple families praised the "green grassy oasis park" surrounded by high-rises, easy walking/cycling access along the foreshore to Surfers Paradise and towards Sea World. Limited bushland but the beachfront location is the standout natural feature.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback about "spotless," "immaculate," and "near new" amenities with "best bathrooms on the east coast." Recent reviews consistently mention modern, clean facilities, excellent laundry, and well-manicured grounds. One family with infant noted some cigarette butts on tent site, and another mentioned kitchen/showers needing more cleaning, preventing a perfect score.

Value (12/15) — Reviews are mixed on value with many stating "very reasonably priced compared to other parks on the Gold Coast" and "good value for money" considering the prime location. However, several noted "pricey especially over Xmas," rates are "up there," and one review mentioned tight sites don't justify premium pricing in this location.

Site Size (6/10) — Consistent feedback that sites are "tight," "packed," "close together," with "little room to move around" even for smaller vans. Multiple reviews mention difficulty backing in larger vans and sites being "sardines." Grass sites with astroturf at doors are noted, but the compact nature is a clear compromise for the urban beachfront location.

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong positive sentiment with families returning multiple times, booking repeat visits, and stating "best caravan park experience on the Gold Coast." Praise for exceptional staff (Nicky, Alan, Jo) going above and beyond. A few negative reviews about cabin quality, noise from construction, and management being "over the job" regarding e-scooter restrictions prevent a perfect score.

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for location with universal praise for being "right across from the beach," walking distance to Tedder Avenue cafes/restaurants, 10-minute walk to tram, bus stop at entrance, and central to Surfers Paradise and Sea World. Families love not needing to drive anywhere with "everything within your reach."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site sizes are consistently described as tight, cramped, and challenging for larger rigs, with limited space between caravans creating a "sardines" feeling that compromises comfort for families wanting outdoor space.

2. Limited on-site kids entertainment infrastructure after playground removal, with only pool and games room mentioned, and no water slides, jumping pillow, or structured kids club that families expect from top-tier holiday parks.

3. Cabin accommodation received poor reviews citing old uncomfortable beds, broken air conditioning, maintenance issues, and poor staff reception, suggesting inconsistent quality across accommodation types and management responsiveness to cabin guests.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"perfect for families," "spotless amenities," "right across from the beach," "well-equipped games room," "Easter activities for the kids," "smoke-free, incredibly quiet," "sites are tight," "best caravan park experience on the Gold Coast"

rationale_top3: Entertainment & Activities (18/20): Outstanding facilities including heated pool, jumping pillow, playground, and food trucks on site providing excellent family entertainment options Cleanliness & Amenities (13/15): Spotless facilities with regular cleaning schedules maintaining consistently high standards throughout the park Natural Environment (13/15): Lovely shady trees and abundant green space creating a relaxed, natural atmosphere with ample room for outdoor activities
rationale_honourable: Positive Guest Sentiment (13/15): Consistently praised for friendly and helpful staff, creating a welcoming atmosphere for all visitors Generous Site Sizing (9/10): Spacious sites providing comfort and privacy, particularly appreciated by guests travelling with pets and families
water_fun: Heated pool, clean but no toddler area
kids_play: Jumping pillow, small playground, limited equipment
pet_detail: Dogs allowed with complimentary dog wash token on arrival, vaccination certificates required, dogs not permitted on Kirra Beach itself.
best_for: Pet-owning families who prioritize beach proximity and spacious sites over cutting-edge facilities and who can tolerate early morning airport noise.
executive_summary:
PARK: Kirra Beach Tourist Park
SCORE: 73/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 260

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Limited onsite entertainment with a jumping pillow and small playground mentioned, plus pool table and table tennis in rec room; food trucks on various nights are a highlight; basic facilities compared to resort-style parks.
Nature (16/20) — Excellent proximity to Kirra Beach (10-minute walk), beautiful paperbarks and shady trees throughout park, coastal walking tracks nearby, beach access is a major drawcard though dogs not allowed on main beach.
Cleanliness (13/15) — Generally clean amenities with regular cleaning noted, though some reviews mention dated facilities, occasional maintenance issues, inconsistent mowing, and shower blocks needing refurbishment.
Value (10/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing with many noting peak season rates of $70-80/night feel expensive for aging facilities; some cite good value while others say overpriced compared to alternatives.
Site Size (9/10) — Consistently praised for spacious, large powered sites with room for big rigs and vehicles; good spacing between sites; easy access for caravans.
Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitation and many 5-star reviews, though sentiment divided on staff friendliness, some security concerns, and frustration with inflexible policies; overall positive but not unanimous.
Location (9/10) — Prime beachside position walking distance to Kirra Beach, cafes, shops; excellent highway access; proximity to airport is double-edged (convenient but noisy from 6am).

WHY NOT 100:
Aircraft noise from Gold Coast Airport significantly impacts guest experience with planes taking off from 6am, mentioned repeatedly as disturbing sleep and ambiance. Aging infrastructure throughout with dated amenities, showers needing upgrades, poor drainage, and maintenance issues noted by long-term repeat visitors. Safety concerns raised including bike thefts, car theft incident, broken glass in play areas, and visible homeless encampment behind park affecting perceived security.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotless amenities", "short walk to the beach", "dog friendly", "spacious sites", "planes taking off at 6am", "amenities are dated", "food trucks", "friendly staff", "heated pool", "no WiFi available", "showers need upgrade", "great location"

rationale_top3: Prime beachfront location just 100m from South Burleigh beach with easy access to Burleigh National Park and coastal walks Exceptionally clean amenities and well-maintained facilities with friendly, helpful staff consistently praised by guests Spacious sites providing plenty of room for families and children to move around comfortably
rationale_honourable: Strong nature and entertainment offerings with kids' activities room and proximity to natural attractions, providing good value despite premium location
kids_play: TV/rec room with games; kids roam
best_for: Beach-loving families with young children who want surf and sand at their doorstep rather than pool-based entertainment.
executive_summary:
PARK: Burleigh Beach Tourist Park
SCORE: 73/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 67

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Limited on-site entertainment with a kids TV/rec room for games and activities during holidays; no pool or waterpark; kids reported riding bikes around the park; one reviewer noted "would be a 10/10 if there was a pool for the kids"; entertainment zone mentioned but minimal structured programs; families rely heavily on beach activities just across the road.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting with direct access to Burleigh Beach (100m away), Burleigh Head National Park walking tracks, and coastal paths to Tallebudgera; "wonderful coastal walks," "stunning views of the ocean," "best sunset you'll see on the Coast"; families praised the park/beach combination for children; "when you're on the beach's doorstep, you won't need" a pool.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Consistently described as "very clean amenities," "always clean and tidy," "super well maintained"; some concerns emerged with "damaged floors and walls," "pots and pans filthy," "walls inside our villa could do with a good scrub"; older facilities noted as "quite dated" but functional; "amenities are some of the best you'll find" though "could also be cleaner."

Value (12/15) — Mixed value perception with "very reasonable tariff" and "value for money" mentioned positively; "dose get expensive in peak times"; one reviewer felt "expect more for the price" given maintenance issues; location premium acknowledged; overall solid value for beachfront position but not exceptional.

Site Size (7/10) — "Large sites," "spacious," "plenty of room for kids to roam around," "sites are roomy and easy to get into and out of"; however multiple mentions of "very tight camp sites," "roads were extremely narrow," "struggled to get our 21ft caravan out"; concrete slabs noted but space constraints evident for larger rigs.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive repeat visitor sentiment: "our go-to place," "stay here all the time," "our favourite Gold Coast tourist park," "will be back for sure" appeared frequently; "love, love, love this place"; exceptional staff praise ("managers go above and beyond"); minor deductions for roadwork noise complaints and maintenance concerns.

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for "best location on the Gold Coast," "across the road from the beach," "walking distance to multiple cafes, shops, restaurants"; "right in the heart of Burleigh Heads"; easy access to theme parks via public transport; "5-minute walk to the beach"; central to all Gold Coast attractions while maintaining quiet park atmosphere.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No swimming pool or water play facilities — multiple families specifically noted "would be a 10/10 if there was a pool for the kids" and this significantly impacts entertainment value for families with young children who want on-site water activities.
2. Limited on-site kids entertainment infrastructure — only a basic TV/rec room with games; no structured programs, waterpark, jumping pillow, or daily activities that families expect from premium holiday parks.
3. Tight site configurations and narrow internal roads — several reviewers with larger caravans (21ft+) struggled with access and maneuvering; "very tight camp sites" impacts the comfort level for families with bigger rigs and reduces site size appeal.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best location in all of burleigh," "wonderful great clean safe campground," "across the road from the beach," "would be a 10/10 if there was a pool for the kids," "very clean amenities," "plenty of room for kids to roam around," "our favourite Gold Coast tourist park," "very tight camp sites"

rationale_top3: Entertainment facilities score strongly with heated pool featuring a popular large waterslide that consistently delights children Prime Gold Coast location provides convenient access to shops, Miami markets, and major theme parks while maintaining affordable pricing Friendly staff and well-maintained sites create a welcoming atmosphere despite some facility age concerns
rationale_honourable: Positive guest sentiment reflects appreciation for the family-focused amenities and value proposition, particularly among return visitors who prioritize location and pool facilities over premium accommodation finishes
water_fun: Large heated pool with impressive waterslide
kids_play: Multiple playgrounds including ninja course available
best_for: Budget-conscious families with younger children who want a pool-focused stay near beaches and theme parks without high cleanliness expectations.
executive_summary:
PARK: Nobby Beach Holiday Village
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-14
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 203

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (15/20) — The standout feature is the large heated pool with an impressive waterslide that kids absolutely love, plus ninja playground and monkey bars. Some events like live music, face painting, and food trucks occur during peak periods. However, slide hours are limited to office hours which frustrated early-rising families, and supervision is lacking.

Nature (11/20) — Located about 10-15 minutes walk from Nobby Beach with reasonable beach access. The park itself has minimal natural features with very few trees and limited green space. Situated on busy Gold Coast Highway under flight path, so natural ambience is compromised. Proximity to Miami Markets and cafes provides some environmental interest.

Cleanliness (8/15) — This is a major concern with highly polarized feedback. Multiple recent reviews describe filthy accommodations with cockroach infestations, dust, grime, sticky floors, and blood stains. Amenities are frequently described as "disgusting," "filthy," "dirty from lunch time on," with toilets not cleaned for days. Some guests report clean experiences, but negative cleanliness reviews are too frequent and severe to ignore.

Value (10/15) — Mixed value perception with many calling it "ridiculously overpriced" especially at $450-500/night for unclean villas. Gold Coast pricing means not cheap for powered sites ($60-80). Some found it "good value" for basic studios and appreciated free upgrades. Location adds value but poor cleanliness significantly undermines it for families expecting holiday park standards.

Site Size (5/10) — Consistent criticism about "very tight space for vans," "super close next to each other," "very cramped," and "could shake hands with neighbor." Caravan sites described as squeezed together with minimal privacy and difficult maneuvering. Some corner sites work better for long rigs. Parking also problematic with limited spaces.

Sentiment (6/10) — Deeply divided sentiment from "absolutely loved everything" to "worst experience ever." Many families return repeatedly and recommend it, praising staff and facilities. However, numerous 1-star reviews express disappointment, anger over cleanliness/cockroaches, poor service, and feeling misled. Recent reviews trend more negative than older ones.

Location (7/10) — Excellent proximity to beaches, Miami Markets (7-minute walk), shops, cafes, and public transport. Central to theme parks (15-30 minutes). Light rail extension coming. However, on busy highway with construction noise, tight entry/exit especially with tram works, and under airport flight path. Location is convenient but not peaceful.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe and recurring cleanliness issues across accommodations and amenities, with multiple reports of cockroach infestations, filthy bathrooms, dusty surfaces, and inadequate cleaning between guests that fundamentally undermine the family holiday experience.
2. Extremely tight site spacing for caravans with minimal privacy, uneven/muddy ground conditions, and difficult vehicle access that makes the camping experience cramped and stressful, particularly for larger rigs.
3. Inconsistent service quality with reports of rude/unhelpful staff, unreturned phone calls and emails, poor communication, inadequate response to serious maintenance issues, and billing/refund problems that erode trust and guest satisfaction.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids loved the pool and the big slide", "absolutely infested with cockroaches", "showers and toilets were absolutely disgusting", "Very tight space for vans", "Staff go above and beyond", "ridiculously overpriced for the disgusting state", "Great location, especially to walk to the Marketta Markets", "amenities really need updating", "received a free upgrade", "ruined our holidays", "Pool area & slide top notch", "place is in desperate need of a DEEP clean"

rationale_top3: Cleanliness Score (11/20): Modern, well-maintained amenities and bathrooms consistently praised by guests Nature Score (10/20): Waterfront location providing direct access to boating and fishing opportunities Entertainment Score (8/20): Limited organized activities but coffee van on-site and proximity to water-based recreation
rationale_honourable: Sentiment Score (8/20): Generally positive guest experiences particularly among boating enthusiasts and pet owners who appreciate the dog-friendly facilities including off-leash area
water_fun: Swimming enclosure nearby, boat ramp adjacent
pet_detail: Dogs allowed with off-leash area, dog treats provided, cats permitted, dog-friendly beaches nearby
best_for: Boating families with dogs visiting during winter months who value waterside location and large sites over children's entertainment
executive_summary:
PARK: Jacobs Well Tourist Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 112

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Reviews consistently note "nothing for kids" on-site with no playground, pool, or organized activities mentioned; families rely entirely on nearby boat ramp and fishing for entertainment

Nature (14/20) — Waterside location with beautiful river views, abundant bird life, netted swimming enclosure, boat ramp access, and surrounded by sugar cane fields; however, natural beauty is severely compromised by "plague proportions" of midges and sandflies that drove multiple families to leave early

Cleanliness (11/15) — Modern amenities described as "very clean" and "cleaned daily" with excellent bathroom facilities; however, cabins received multiple complaints about poor cleaning, dust under beds, insufficient supplies, and "bird and bat lice in excess" in some areas

Value (8/15) — Frequent complaints about pricing: "$90 a night for an unpowered site", "not worth the nightly tariff of $60", "very very expensive for a camp ground", "over priced"; waterside sites noted at $50 for two adults but value undermined by insect issues forcing early departures

Site Size (8/10) — Multiple reviews praise "really quite large powered van sites", "one of the biggest site caravan parks we have stayed", and "good sized sites"; some complaints about tight squeezes and sites on dirt/sand rather than grass

Sentiment (6/10) — Mixed sentiment with loyal repeat visitors balanced by multiple "never going back" declarations; insect problems, noise from adjacent pub and parking area with "rude, obnoxious youth", and inconsistent management response damaged overall satisfaction

Location (5/10) — Convenient location "close to everything" with shops, pub, bakery across the road, 20 minutes to Pimpama shopping, under an hour to Brisbane/Gold Coast; however, adjacent parking area creates safety and noise concerns with "speeding cars and motorbikes all night", police presence, and pub music until midnight on weekends

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe and persistent midge/sandfly infestations caused multiple families to leave days early despite using repellents, with reviews describing "plague proportions", "30 bites each in one night", and "absolutely smashed from the bloody mosquitoes and midges" — this single issue dominated negative feedback
2. Complete absence of on-site children's entertainment, playground, or pool facilities, with multiple reviews explicitly stating "nothing for kids", limiting appeal to families with young children who need structured activities
3. Significant noise, safety, and cleanliness inconsistencies including adjacent parking area disturbances with intoxicated individuals, cabin cleaning issues (dust, cockroaches, insufficient supplies), and management complaints about site allocation, excessive security codes, and poor customer service responses

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"absolutely smashed from the bloody mosquitoes and midges", "park has sandflies and midges in plague proportions", "30 bites each in one night", "left 2 days early due to the biting insects", "nothing for kids", "one of the biggest site caravan parks we have stayed", "amenities are clean and well maintained", "noisy, not safe and over priced", "cabin not very clean", "absolutely amazing little dog friendly park", "not worth the nightly tariff", "midgee heaven"
